overview: The BMO Canadian Dividend ETF (ZDV.TO) provides exposure to a diversified portfolio of high-dividend-paying Canadian stocks. The ETF focuses on companies with strong dividend histories and sustainable payout ratios, primarily across financials, energy, utilities, and telecoms. With a yield of approximately 4-5% and an MER of 0.39%, ZDV offers a balance between income generation and cost efficiency. It holds a broader set of dividend payers than bank-only ETFs like ZEB, providing more sector diversification within the Canadian dividend space.
bullCase: Attractive yield of approximately 4-5% from diversified Canadian dividend payers.
Broader sector exposure than bank-focused ETFs — includes energy, utilities, telecoms, and pipelines.
Reasonable MER of 0.39% for an actively screened dividend strategy.
bearCase: 100% Canadian exposure means no international diversification.
Dividend cuts from underlying holdings can reduce income, especially during economic downturns.
Higher MER than passive broad-market alternatives like XIC (0.06%).
analystSummary: ZDV is a solid Canadian dividend ETF at 0.39% MER offering ~4-5% yield across financials, energy, utilities, and telecoms. More diversified than bank-only ETFs, it suits income investors wanting broad Canadian dividend exposure.
seoDescription: Explore our ZDV ETF analysis — BMO Canadian Dividend ETF with ~4-5% yield at 0.39% MER across Canada's top dividend sectors.
